mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals strong economic demand, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 60%+ of quarters with GDPC1 growth > 1.5% YoY despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5.0% indicates weakening labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0% despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL signal tightening monetary policy amid inflation, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of periods where both FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL > 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average indicate weakening supply-side capacity, which reduces corporate profitability and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO falls below its 12-month MA.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) above 1.2 million units signals increased construction activity and consumer confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 60%+ of periods where HOUST exceeds 1.2M.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 100k growth indicates labor market weakness, which reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS growth falls below 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate robust demand conditions, which drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 60%+ of periods where both RSAFS exceeds 450B and HOUST exceeds 1.1M.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 1.0% signals monetary easing, which lowers discount rates and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 60%+ of periods where FEDFUNDS falls below 1.0%.
